Name: Job Search RAG
Description: Local LLM + RAG pipeline filtering jobs across boards using semantic search.

JobBoards.Description: Table: JobBoards - Configured job board sources that can be crawled for listings.

schema.name: JobBoardId
schema.Description: Primary key for job boards.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display name of the job board (e.g., ZipRecruiter, Indeed, LinkedIn, WeWorkRemotely).

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Notes about this board's behavior, rate limits, or special handling requirements.

schema.name: IsEnabled
schema.Description: Whether this board is active for search runs.

schema.name: MaxPages
schema.Description: Maximum number of result pages to crawl per search URL. Default is 3.

schema.name: IsHeadless
schema.Description: Whether to run the Playwright browser in headless mode for this board.

schema.name: BrowserChannel
schema.Description: Optional browser variant to use (e.g., msedge). NULL means default Chromium.

schema.name: JobListings
schema.Description: Count of job listings scraped from this board.

data.Name: ZipRecruiter
data.Description: Primary board. Uses Next.js SERP structure. Full adapter implemented.

data.Name: LinkedIn
data.Description: Overnight mode recommended. Stealth browsing, throttled requests. Stricter bot detection.

data.Name: WeWorkRemotely
data.Description: Remote-focused board. Smaller volume, higher signal for remote roles.

SearchUrls.Description: Table: SearchUrls - Individual search URLs configured per board, each representing a specific query to crawl.

schema.name: SearchUrlId
schema.Description: Primary key for search URLs.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Descriptive label for this search query (e.g., 'Staff Engineer - Remote').

schema.name: JobBoard
schema.Description: Which job board this search URL belongs to.

schema.name: Url
schema.Description: The full search URL to crawl on this board.

schema.name: IsEnabled
schema.Description: Whether this search URL is active for crawling.

schema.name: JobBoardName
schema.Description: Display name of the parent job board.

RoleArchetypes.Description: Table: RoleArchetypes - Target role descriptions that define ideal job types for semantic matching.

schema.name: RoleArchetypeId
schema.Description: Primary key for role archetypes.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Archetype title (e.g., 'Staff Platform Architect', 'AI Systems Engineer').

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Natural language description of this ideal role type. Embedded into ChromaDB for archetype scoring.

schema.name: SignalsPositive
schema.Description: Comma-separated keywords or phrases indicating strong fit for this archetype.

schema.name: SignalsNegative
schema.Description: Comma-separated keywords or phrases indicating misfit for this archetype.

data.Name: AI Systems Engineer / AI Platform Architect
data.Description: Designs and builds production ML/AI infrastructure: model serving, training pipelines, vector stores, embedding services, and LLM orchestration layers.
data.SignalsPositive: ML infrastructure, model serving, training pipeline, vector database, LLM orchestration, embedding service, MLOps, GPU cluster
data.SignalsNegative: frontend only, marketing automation, junior data analyst, manual QA

data.Name: Staff Platform Architect
data.Description: Cross-team technical leadership for platform and infrastructure. Drives architectural decisions, defines system boundaries, and mentors engineering teams.
data.SignalsPositive: platform architecture, system design, cross-team influence, technical strategy, API design, distributed systems, mentorship
data.SignalsNegative: individual contributor only, no architecture scope, ticket taker, single-team

data.Name: Principal Data Platform Engineer
data.Description: Owns data infrastructure at scale: data pipelines, warehousing, streaming, and analytical platform services.
data.SignalsPositive: data platform, data pipeline, streaming, data warehouse, Spark, Kafka, Flink, dbt, analytical infrastructure
data.SignalsNegative: BI analyst, dashboard builder, Excel reporting, entry-level data

data.Name: Developer Relations / Technical Evangelist
data.Description: External-facing technical role bridging engineering and developer community. Produces technical content, speaks at conferences, and drives API/SDK adoption.
data.SignalsPositive: developer relations, developer advocacy, technical writing, conference speaking, SDK adoption, API evangelism, community building
data.SignalsNegative: sales quota, cold calling, non-technical marketing, pure product management

RubricDimensions.Description: Table: RubricDimensions - Universal evaluation criteria applied to all listings for culture and quality scoring.

schema.name: RubricDimensionId
schema.Description: Primary key for rubric dimensions.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Dimension name (e.g., 'Role Scope', 'Technical Depth').

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What this dimension evaluates and why it matters.

schema.name: SignalsPositive
schema.Description: Comma-separated positive indicators for this dimension. Embedded into global_positive_signals collection.

schema.name: SignalsNegative
schema.Description: Comma-separated red flags for this dimension. Embedded into negative_signals collection.

data.Name: Role Scope
data.Description: Cross-team influence and architectural reach vs. narrow single-team execution.
data.SignalsPositive: cross-team, architectural influence, system design ownership, technical strategy
data.SignalsNegative: ticket taker, narrow scope, single feature team, no design authority

data.Name: Technical Depth
data.Description: Infrastructure and platform work vs. web/feature dev.
data.SignalsPositive: infrastructure, platform, distributed systems, systems programming, performance engineering
data.SignalsNegative: WordPress, Squarespace, basic CRUD, landing pages, no-code

data.Name: Operational Burden
data.Description: On-call/SRE load vs. platform engineering focus.
data.SignalsPositive: platform engineering, build tools, developer experience, automation
data.SignalsNegative: 24/7 on-call, pager duty primary, incident commander rotation, SRE-heavy

data.Name: Industry Alignment
data.Description: Company mission and product category alignment with candidate interests.
data.SignalsPositive: developer tools, AI/ML, data infrastructure, open source, cloud platform

data.Name: Employment Structure
data.Description: Direct hire vs. staffing agency/contract positions.
data.SignalsNegative: staffing agency, contract-to-hire, vendor chain, W2 through third party, corp-to-corp

data.Name: Seniority Mismatch
data.Description: Role level vs. candidate expectations for staff+ positions.
data.SignalsPositive: staff, principal, distinguished, fellow, architect, VP engineering, director
data.SignalsNegative: junior, entry-level, associate, new grad, 0-2 years experience, intern

data.Name: Compensation Red Flags
data.Description: Compensation transparency and alignment with staff/principal market expectations.
data.SignalsPositive: transparent compensation range, top-of-range meets target minimum, staff/principal market rate
data.SignalsNegative: equity-only compensation, unpaid or volunteer, below market for principal-level, hourly contractor rate far below salary

data.Name: Culture & Work Model
data.Description: Remote-first culture, work-life balance, and engineering autonomy indicators.
data.SignalsPositive: remote-first, async-first, work-life balance, engineering autonomy, written communication culture, high-trust low-chaos
data.SignalsNegative: mandatory return-to-office, 4+ days on-site, 60+ hour weeks, military-style command, fast-paced high-pressure, ambiguous expectations

data.Name: Ethical Alignment
data.Description: Company mission alignment with humane and privacy-respecting values.
data.SignalsPositive: mission-driven, humane impact, privacy-respecting, healthcare, education, public-good
data.SignalsNegative: weapons manufacturing, military contracting, surveillance technology, behavioral profiling, predatory lending

data.Name: Neurodivergence Compatibility
data.Description: Structured processes, predictable workflows, and low-meeting culture for neurodivergent-friendly work environments.
data.SignalsPositive: clear communication norms, structured processes, predictable workflows, low-meeting culture, async-first, psychological safety
data.SignalsNegative: constant context switching, interrupt-driven, ambiguity without support, high-meeting culture, synchronous dependency, chaotic environment

Resumes.Description: Table: Resumes - Candidate resumes that anchor the entire RAG scoring pipeline. Every search run scores job listings against a specific resume's embeddings.

schema.name: ResumeId
schema.Description: Primary key for resumes.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display name for this resume version (e.g., 'Platform Engineer - 2026Q1').

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Notes about what this resume version emphasizes or targets.

schema.name: FilePath
schema.Description: Path to the resume markdown file relative to project root (e.g., 'data/resume.md').

schema.name: IndexedAt
schema.Description: When this resume was last chunked and embedded into ChromaDB. NULL means not yet indexed.

schema.name: ResumeSections
schema.Description: Count of sections (## heading chunks) extracted from this resume.

schema.name: SearchRuns
schema.Description: Count of search runs that scored against this resume.

data.Name: Platform Engineer - 2026 Q1
data.Description: Emphasizes distributed systems, platform architecture, and AI/ML infrastructure. Targets staff+ IC and architect roles.

ResumeSections.Description: Table: ResumeSections - Individual chunks of a resume, split by ## headings. Each section is embedded separately into ChromaDB for granular semantic matching against job descriptions.

schema.name: ResumeSectionId
schema.Description: Primary key. Slug of the section heading (e.g., 'resume-core-strengths').

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Section heading text (e.g., 'Core Strengths', 'Experience').

schema.name: Resume
schema.Description: Which resume this section belongs to.

schema.name: SectionOrder
schema.Description: Order of this section within the resume (1-based). Preserves document structure.

schema.name: Content
schema.Description: Full text of this section including the ## heading and all body content up to the next ## heading. This is the document embedded into ChromaDB.

schema.name: ChromaDocId
schema.Description: Document ID in the ChromaDB resume collection (e.g., 'resume-core-strengths'). Used for idempotent upserts.

schema.name: ResumeName
schema.Description: Display name of the parent resume.

data.Name: Summary
data.Content:
## Summary
Senior platform engineer with 15+ years building distributed systems, data pipelines, and AI/ML infrastructure. Track record of cross-team technical leadership at scale.

data.Name: Core Strengths
data.Content:
## Core Strengths
- Distributed systems architecture (Kubernetes, gRPC, service mesh)
- ML/AI platform engineering (model serving, training pipelines, vector stores)
- Technical strategy and mentorship across 50+ engineer orgs
- Data infrastructure (Kafka, Spark, Flink, dbt)

data.Name: Experience
data.Content:
## Experience
### Staff Platform Engineer — CloudScale Inc (2022-2026)
Led platform architecture for ML serving infrastructure. Designed multi-region Kubernetes deployment strategy. Reduced model inference latency 40%.
### Senior Engineer — DataForge (2018-2022)
Built real-time data pipelines processing 2M events/sec. Migrated monolith to event-driven microservices.

data.Name: Earlier Roles
data.Content:
## Earlier Roles
### Software Engineer — StartupCo (2014-2018)
Full-stack development, API design, early DevOps adoption.
### Junior Developer — Agency LLC (2011-2014)
Web application development, client projects, learned software engineering fundamentals.

SearchRuns.Description: Table: SearchRuns - Individual pipeline execution runs that crawl boards, score listings, and produce ranked results. Each run scores against a specific resume.

schema.name: SearchRunId
schema.Description: Primary key for search runs.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable run label (typically date + time).

schema.name: Resume
schema.Description: Which resume was used for fit_score computation in this run. The central link in the RAG pipeline.

schema.name: RunDate
schema.Description: When this search run was executed.

schema.name: ArchetypeWeight
schema.Description: Weight applied to archetype match score (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: FitWeight
schema.Description: Weight applied to resume fit score (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: HistoryWeight
schema.Description: Weight applied to decision history score (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: CompWeight
schema.Description: Weight applied to compensation alignment score (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: NegativeWeight
schema.Description: Penalty multiplier for negative signal score (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: CultureWeight
schema.Description: Weight applied to culture/quality dimension score (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: BaseSalary
schema.Description: Target annual salary for compensation scoring. Default $220,000.

schema.name: MinScoreThreshold
schema.Description: Minimum final score to include in results (0.0 - 1.0). Default 0.45.

schema.name: DisqualifyOnLlmFlag
schema.Description: Whether to run the LLM disqualifier pass on listings.

schema.name: TotalFound
schema.Description: Total listings collected from boards in this run.

schema.name: TotalScored
schema.Description: Total listings that passed through the scorer.

schema.name: TotalExcluded
schema.Description: Listings filtered below min_score_threshold.

schema.name: TotalDeduplicated
schema.Description: Duplicate listings collapsed during this run.

schema.name: FailedListings
schema.Description: Count of listings that failed scoring.

schema.name: ResumeName
schema.Description: Display name of the resume used for this run.

schema.name: ScoreResults
schema.Description: Count of score results produced in this run.

JobListings.Description: Table: JobListings - Individual job postings scraped from job boards, normalized to a common data contract.
JobListings.summary_rich: The **postings pinned to the corkboard**. Every listing is normalized from its source board into the same shape — title, company, location, full description text, comp band — so downstream RAG scoring doesn't care whether it came from ZipRecruiter or WeWorkRemotely. `FullText` is the document that gets chunked and embedded into ChromaDB; everything else is metadata for filtering and display.

schema.name: JobListingId
schema.Description: Primary key. Composite of board and external ID (e.g., 'ziprecruiter-abc123').

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Job title as listed on the board, sanitized for filesystem safety.

schema.name: JobBoard
schema.Description: Which job board this listing was scraped from.

schema.name: ExternalId
schema.Description: The job board's unique identifier for this posting.

schema.name: Company
schema.Description: Company name, sanitized for filesystem safety.

schema.name: Location
schema.Description: Job location (city, state, remote, hybrid, etc.).

schema.name: Url
schema.Description: Direct URL link to the job posting on the board.

schema.name: FullText
schema.Description: Complete job description text. Max 250,000 characters. Embedded into ChromaDB for scoring.

schema.name: PostedAt
schema.Description: When the job was posted on the board.

schema.name: CompMin
schema.Description: Minimum compensation in USD, parsed from job description.

schema.name: CompMax
schema.Description: Maximum compensation in USD, parsed from job description.

schema.name: CompSource
schema.Description: Source of compensation data: 'employer' (from JD) or 'estimated' (board-generated).

schema.name: CompText
schema.Description: Raw compensation text matched from the job description (e.g., '$180,000 - $220,000/year').

schema.name: JobBoardName
schema.Description: Display name of the source job board.
schema.explanation_rich: **One listing, one board, one normalized name.** ZipRecruiter calls its identifier `ziprecruiter`, WeWorkRemotely calls its identifier `weworkremotely` — but the corkboard wants a human-readable label. This lookup chases the `JobBoard` FK back into `JobBoards` and pulls the display `Name`. Worked example: a scraped row with `JobBoard='weworkremotely'` resolves to `JobBoardName='WeWorkRemotely'`; rename the board to *'We Work Remotely'* in `JobBoards` and *every* listing card on the corkboard updates the next time the rulebook recomputes — no migration, no UI patch.

schema.name: ScoreResults
schema.Description: Number of times this listing has been scored across search runs.

schema.name: Decisions
schema.Description: Number of decisions recorded for this listing.

ScoreResults.Description: Table: ScoreResults - Component and final scores for a job listing within a specific search run.
ScoreResults.summary_rich: The **scoring grid** — every (listing, run) pair gets a row recording its six component scores (fit, archetype, history, comp, culture, negative) and the fused `FinalScore`. This is where the RAG signal becomes a number: cosine distance from resume embeddings becomes `FitScore`, role-archetype proximity becomes `ArchetypeScore`, comp-band alignment becomes `CompScore`, and the LLM disqualifier can zero the whole row.

schema.name: ScoreResultId
schema.Description: Primary key for score results.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label combining job title and search run.

schema.name: JobListing
schema.Description: The job listing that was scored.

schema.name: SearchRun
schema.Description: The search run during which this score was produced.

schema.name: FitScore
schema.Description: Semantic similarity to resume (0.0 - 1.0). Based on cosine distance in resume ChromaDB collection.

schema.name: ArchetypeScore
schema.Description: Match to role archetypes (0.0 - 1.0). Based on cosine distance in role_archetypes collection.

schema.name: HistoryScore
schema.Description: Match to past accepted decisions (0.0 - 1.0). Only 'yes' verdicts contribute.

schema.name: CompScore
schema.Description: Compensation alignment with base salary (0.0 - 1.0). Non-linear curve; 0.5 when comp is missing.

schema.name: NegativeScore
schema.Description: Penalty score from negative signal matches (0.0 - 1.0). Subtracted from positive scores.

schema.name: CultureScore
schema.Description: Match to positive culture/quality dimensions (0.0 - 1.0).

schema.name: IsDisqualified
schema.Description: Whether the LLM disqualifier flagged this listing. If true, final score is forced to 0.0.

schema.name: DisqualifierReason
schema.Description: Free-text explanation from the LLM disqualifier about why the role was flagged.

schema.name: FinalScore
schema.Description: Weighted fusion of all component scores. Zeroed if disqualified. Formula: max(0, positive_sum - negative_weight * negative_score).
schema.explanation_rich: **The one number that decides whether a posting reaches the corkboard.** Five positive signals (fit, archetype, history, comp, culture) are summed, the negative-signal penalty is subtracted, the floor is clamped to zero — and if the LLM disqualifier flagged the row, the whole thing collapses to `0` regardless. Worked example: a *Staff Platform Engineer* listing scores `FitScore=0.78` (resume cosine match), `ArchetypeScore=0.71` (staff-architect archetype), `HistoryScore=0.40` (similar to a past 'yes'), `CompScore=0.65`, `CultureScore=0.55`, `NegativeScore=0.12` (mild 'on-call rotation' penalty), `IsDisqualified=FALSE`. Final = `0.78 + 0.71 + 0.40 + 0.65 + 0.55 − 0.12 = 2.97` — well above the `0.45` threshold. Flip `IsDisqualified` to `TRUE` (LLM detected 'staffing agency') and the same row collapses to `0.0` the next read.

schema.name: DuplicateBoards
schema.Description: Comma-separated list of other boards where this same job appeared (cross-board deduplication).

schema.name: JobListingName
schema.Description: Title of the scored job listing.

schema.name: SearchRunName
schema.Description: Label of the parent search run.

schema.name: Company
schema.Description: Company name from the scored listing.

Decisions.Description: Table: Decisions - Operator verdicts on reviewed job listings, used as training signal for future scoring.

schema.name: DecisionId
schema.Description: Primary key for decisions.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label combining verdict and job title.

schema.name: JobListing
schema.Description: The job listing this decision is about.

schema.name: Verdict
schema.Description: Operator's judgment: 'yes' (interested), 'no' (rejected), 'maybe' (uncertain), or 'removed' (retracted).

schema.name: Reason
schema.Description: Free-text explanation for the verdict. Stored for audit purposes.

schema.name: RecordedAt
schema.Description: Timestamp when this decision was recorded.

schema.name: IsScoringSignal
schema.Description: Whether this verdict contributes to history_score. Only 'yes' verdicts are scoring signals.
schema.explanation_rich: **The RAG loop only learns from things you said yes to.** Every operator verdict gets a row — `yes`, `no`, `maybe`, `removed` — but only the `yes` rows get embedded back into ChromaDB's `accepted_decisions` collection where future runs compute `HistoryScore` from cosine proximity. Worked example: you mark *CloudScale's Staff Platform Engineer* as `yes` → `IsScoringSignal=TRUE` → its `FullText` joins the positive history. Next run's *DataForge Principal Architect* listing has high textual overlap → its `HistoryScore` jumps. Mark something `maybe` and `IsScoringSignal=FALSE` — it's audited but never feeds the retrieval index. Flip the same verdict to `yes` later and the very next search run picks it up.

schema.name: JobListingName
schema.Description: Title of the decided job listing.

schema.name: Company
schema.Description: Company name from the decided listing.

schema.name: Board
schema.Description: Job board the decided listing came from.

__meta__.Description: Project-level metadata that travels with the rulebook: tagline, motif, narrative descriptions, substrate list, signature rows, etc. One row per metadata key. Use ValueType to interpret StringValue vs JsonValue.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: The metadata key (e.g. 'tagline', 'motif_palette', 'substrates'). Unique within the table.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Identifier for this metadata entry. Mirrors MetaKey so the row is addressable by Name like every other table.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: How to interpret the value columns: 'string' (use StringValue), 'object' (parse JsonValue as JSON object), 'array' (parse JsonValue as JSON array).

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Plain string value. Populated when ValueType == 'string'; null otherwise.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value. Populated when ValueType == 'object' or 'array'; null when ValueType == 'string'.

data.Name: tagline
data.StringValue: Crawls job boards, scores every listing against your resume with RAG, pins the ones worth reading.

data.Name: description_rich
data.StringValue: Job Search RAG is a local LLM + retrieval pipeline modeled as a rulebook. Job boards get crawled, listings get normalized, the resume is chunked and embedded into ChromaDB, and every (listing, run) pair is scored on six dimensions — fit, archetype, history, comp, culture, negative — then fused into a single `FinalScore`. The interesting thing isn't the scraping; it's that every weight, every threshold, every rubric dimension lives in the rulebook as data. Change a `RubricDimension.SignalsNegative` row, and the next search run penalizes those phrases everywhere — across Postgres, Python, and the cosine-distance scorer — without touching a line of pipeline code.

data.Name: use_cases
data.JsonValue: ["**Pin a new role archetype.** Add a row to `RoleArchetypes` (e.g., *'Site Reliability Architect'*) with positive/negative signals and a natural-language description. The next search run embeds it into ChromaDB and starts scoring listings against the new target — no code change.", "**Penalize a phrase you keep seeing.** Edit `RubricDimensions.culture-work-model.SignalsNegative` to add *'4+ days on-site'*. Every previously scored listing's `NegativeScore` updates the next time `FinalScore` recomputes, and below-threshold rows fall off the corkboard.", "**Teach the loop with a 'yes'.** Mark a `Decisions` row as `yes` for *CloudScale's Staff Platform Engineer*. `IsScoringSignal` flips to TRUE; the listing's `FullText` joins the accepted-decisions ChromaDB collection; next run's `HistoryScore` favors textually similar postings.", "**Disqualify a listing without deleting it.** Set `ScoreResults.IsDisqualified=TRUE` with a reason. `FinalScore` collapses to `0.0` the next read; the audit row stays for review; nothing else in the pipeline needs to know.", "**Retune the weighted fusion.** Change `SearchRuns.NegativeWeight` from `1.0` to `1.5` for the next run; the `FinalScore` formula re-fires across every `ScoreResults` row in that run with the harsher penalty — no scorer redeploy."]

data.Name: journal_seed
data.StringValue: One resume indexed, four boards configured (one live), four role archetypes pinned, ten rubric dimensions wired. The corkboard is ready — once a search run lands, every listing will be scored against this resume's embeddings and the operator's accepted decisions.

data.Name: CMCC_Summary
data.StringValue: Rulebook for Job Search RAG - a local LLM + RAG pipeline for intelligent job filtering using semantic search across multiple job boards.

data.Name: conversion_metadata
data.JsonValue: {"source_base_id": "manual", "table_count": 9, "tool_version": "2.0.0", "field_type_mapping": "manual schema design from domain model analysis", "export_mode": "schema_first_type_mapping", "type_inference": {"priority": "manual domain analysis", "error_value_handling": "#NUM!, #ERROR!, #N/A, #REF!, #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #NAME? are treated as NULL"}}
